operational_knowledge_units.title: RobotToteDatabseModel stores tote identity, state, location, and robot assignment
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide states that the RobotToteDatabseModel table contains tote information for all totes in the system, including toteid, tote state, locationid, and robot id.
observed_evidence: Slide text: "The RobotToteDatabseModel table contains the tote information including toteid, the state of the tote, the locationid, and the robot id for all the totes in the system."
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: RobotToteDatabseModel database table reference. In the Robotics Database, the Robot Tote Database Model stores tote information including toteid, tote state, locationid, and robot id for all totes in the system.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot tote model uses enumerated tote lifecycle and routing states
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide lists tote states tracked in the RobotToteDatabseModel, covering request, pickup, delivery to sorter or hospital, induction, and removal conditions.
observed_evidence: Listed states: PickupToteRequested, TotePickedUp, MoveOutSorterRequested, AtTipper, DropOffSorterRequested, DroppedOffSorter, DropOffHospitalRequested, DroppedOffHospital, InductToteRequested, ToteInducted, RemoveToteRequested, ToteRemoved
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot tote states in RobotToteDatabseModel. Tote States listed on the slide are PickupToteRequested, TotePickedUp, MoveOutSorterRequested, AtTipper, DropOffSorterRequested, DroppedOffSorter, DropOffHospitalRequested, DroppedOffHospital, InductToteRequested, ToteInducted, RemoveToteRequested, and ToteRemoved.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot tote state model reflects tote handling requests and completions across destinations
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The observed state names indicate that tote handling is tracked as requested and completed events for pickup, sorter drop-off, hospital drop-off, induction, and removal, with an intermediate AtTipper state also represented.
observed_evidence: Request/completion style state pairs are listed on the slide, including PickupToteRequested/TotePickedUp, DropOffSorterRequested/DroppedOffSorter, DropOffHospitalRequested/DroppedOffHospital, InductToteRequested/ToteInducted, and RemoveToteRequested/ToteRemoved
The state list also includes MoveOutSorterRequested and AtTipper
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: System behavior in the robotics tote database indicates tote handling is modeled with requested and completed states for pickup, sorter drop-off, hospital drop-off, induction, and removal, plus states such as MoveOutSorterRequested and AtTipper.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: No explicit procedure present on Robot Tote Database Model slide
preconditions: Reviewer is examining slide 81 for procedure content
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Slide presents a database model description and a list of tote states, with no numbered or imperative action sequence.
warnings: The slide lists database fields and tote states but does not provide operator or support actions.
No reusable action guidance or exact sequence is explicitly stated.
procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Discarded procedure candidate for slide 81. The Robot Tote Database Model slide contains database context and tote state definitions, not explicit operational steps or instructions.
